pain_point_label: Duplicate Source Systems & Batch Reconciliation Overhead

matches.match_rationale: The core pain is data spread across multiple systems with incompatible schemas (SIS vs portal, payroll vs HRIS, accounting vs campus ledgers). AI-assisted ETL mapping can automatically identify corresponding fields across these systems, normalize formats, and flag conflicting values — reducing the manual reconciliation burden. This directly attacks the 15-20 hours/month wasted identifying which version is authoritative.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A continuous data harmonization layer that ingests records from all source systems, maps them to a canonical schema, flags discrepancies with confidence scores, and surfaces a recommended authoritative value for human confirmation.

matches.match_rationale: When the same entity (student, employee, transaction) has multiple conflicting records across systems, detecting which records diverge from expected patterns is an anomaly detection problem. AI can flag records where values differ beyond acceptable tolerances, prioritizing reconciliation effort on genuine conflicts rather than expected minor variations. This is especially valuable for three-way splits introduced by offline campuses.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated reconciliation monitor that continuously compares records across source systems and generates a daily conflict report ranked by severity — directing staff attention to the highest-risk discrepancies first.

matches.match_rationale: The reconciliation workflow itself — pulling data from multiple systems, comparing records, applying business rules to determine authority, logging decisions, and updating the canonical record — is a multi-step decision workflow ideally suited to a task automation agent. The agent can handle the routine 80% of reconciliation automatically (where rules are clear) and escalate only genuinely ambiguous conflicts to human reviewers. This directly eliminates the 15-20 hours/month manual overhead.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An autonomous reconciliation agent that runs nightly or on-demand, ingests exports from all source systems, applies authority rules (e.g., SIS wins for enrollment status, payroll wins for compensation), auto-resolves non-conflicting duplicates, and produces a human-review queue for genuine conflicts.

matches.match_rationale: After reconciliation cycles complete, operators need to document which records were authoritative, what was changed, and why — for audit trails and compliance. AI text generation can automatically produce reconciliation summaries, change logs, and exception narratives from structured diff data, eliminating the documentation burden that follows each manual reconciliation round.
matches.opportunity_sketch: Auto-generated reconciliation reports that turn system diff logs into plain-English summaries for department heads, showing what changed, what was flagged, and what requires action — reducing documentation time post-reconciliation.
